nodes.label: AI as writing assistant
nodes.grounding_span: Grammarly's built-in AI always helps me fine tune my writing skills

nodes.label: AI as meeting note-taking tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or insufficient for complex tasks
nodes.grounding_span: commonly use as a first level search engine (and to transcribe/take meeting notes)

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: I mostly collaborate with it while I am working throughout a work assignment

nodes.label: AI for administrative tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI for tasks that support professional development
nodes.grounding_span: The kind of tasks I do utilize AI for are for more mundane, administrative in nature

nodes.label: AI as skill development enabler
nodes.label_negative: AI as crutch that hinders learning
nodes.grounding_span: the more opportunities I foresee myself into learning from what the AI produces

nodes.label: AI as data analysis and insight generator
nodes.grounding_span: synthesizing all of the data on the spreadsheet and then creating an overview 'big-picture' look

nodes.label: Professional growth and skill maintenance
nodes.grounding_span: most beneficial for my continued professional development in my current role

nodes.label: Efficiency and cost reduction
nodes.grounding_span: become more operationally efficient with how I handle my business operations workflows

nodes.label: Authenticity and personal touch
nodes.grounding_span: a more 'humanized' touch because there are certain cases where it's pretty comically obvious that AI responded

nodes.label: Career advancement and relevance
nodes.grounding_span: show my manager that I am also capable of taking on more responsibilities in business finance areas

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for meeting notes
nodes.grounding_span: I felt very satisfied with AI was the first time I discovered that the built-in Gemini feature...had the ability to take notes and transcribe work meetings

nodes.label: Frustration with hidden AI use
nodes.grounding_span: The primary times I find frustrating with AI...are the little annoying features that come built into the tool

nodes.label: Strategic preservation of learning tasks from AI
nodes.grounding_span: I mentally factor how much time I would save if I were to punt that task to an AI versus the amount of valuable knowledge I would lose out on

nodes.label: Optimism about AI for career advancement
nodes.grounding_span: The more I familiarize myself with how these AI tools operate the more likely I will want to use it into my daily workflow

nodes.label: Strategic task differentiation — selective AI use based on task type
nodes.grounding_span: The tasks I like to leave for myself to do are ones that will be most beneficial for my continued professional development

nodes.label: Collaborative iterative refinement — works back and forth with AI
nodes.grounding_span: I act as the final editor of whatever it produces because I always double check my work
